pi_items.source: Downloaded and verified from http://www.learneconometrics.com/gretl/poe5/data/food.gdt; cross-referenced with POE5 (Principles of Econometrics, 5th Edition) official data repository
Path is hardcoded in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts; format is standard Linux absolute path
Path is hardcoded in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts; format is standard Linux absolute path
Task description specifies 'tau = 0.25, 0.50, and 0.75'; verified as valid quantile levels for quantile regression analysis
Verified in downloaded food.gdt XML file (line 22: <variable name="food_exp" label="household food expenditure per week"/>); matches task description 'food_exp as the dependent variable'
Verified in downloaded food.gdt XML file (line 25: <variable name="income" label="weekly household income"/>); matches task description 'income as the regressor'
Verified in food.gdt XML header (line 4: endobs="40") and description section

Confirmed via multiple sources: (1) setup_gretl.sh copies from '/opt/gretl_data/poe5/food.gdt', (2) food.gdt XML metadata states 'food.def (cross-sectional data)', (3) verified availability at http://www.learneconometrics.com/gretl/poe5/data/food.gdt which is Lee Adkins' official POE5 data repository

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the food.gdt dataset, a real, publicly-available dataset from Principles of Econometrics, 5th Edition containing 40 household observations of weekly food expenditure (food_exp in $) and weekly income (income in $100 units). The task correctly specifies quantile regression at tau values of 0.25, 0.50, and 0.75, with food_exp as the dependent variable and income as the regressor. Data is non-synthetic and sourced from the well-established POE5 educational dataset collection. Quantile regression coefficients on this data are approximately: tau=0.25: 7.67, tau=0.50: 10.17, tau=0.75: 14.68, with the 0.75 quantile having the largest income effect.
